summary: Decent soundboard recording, but the 1/28‑87 show is marred by shaky performances—only ‘Must Have Been The Roses’ and ‘Row Jimmy’ really shine.
review: The SBD source captures a clear picture of the night, though a few listeners note speed/pitch quirks in the second set. Musically the band was still stumbling out of Jerry’s coma; most reviewers call the show one of the weaker ones of the era, with the exception of a few highlights like ‘Must Have Been The Roses’, ‘Row Jimmy’ and a solid ‘Black Peter’. The recording is listenable, but the performance quality keeps it from being a recommended listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews criticize the musical performance (70% weight) while acknowledging that the soundboard source is fairly clear (30% weight). The mix of very low star ratings with a handful of sentimental high scores yields an overall rating in the low‑mid range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear SBD capture; a few users mention occasional speed/pitch issues on the second set and minor drop‑outs, but overall clarity is decent.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The band struggled to find a groove early on, with the first set feeling directionless and the second set improving only marginally. Many note a lack of cohesion typical of the post‑coma period.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Frequently described as off‑key, mumbling and sluggish; some reviewers still appreciate his presence after the coma.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as surprisingly audible and solid on this night by one reviewer.
band_member_comments.Bob: His vocals are called slurred, lyric‑flubbed and often unintelligible throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) is praised for holding things together; Dan Healy’s vocal effects receive both criticism and occasional praise.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey are generally regarded as competent and steady despite the overall band wobble.
